textline_cells.text: READING TIPS FOR BABIES
textline_cells.orig: READING TIPS FOR BABIES

textline_cells.text: DIVE INTO READING
textline_cells.orig: DIVE INTO READING

textline_cells.text: LONG BEACH PUBLIC LIBRARY
textline_cells.orig: LONG BEACH PUBLIC LIBRARY

textline_cells.text: This information is available in alternative format by calling (562) 570-7500 or visiting www.lbpl.org

textline_cells.text: Try out a few of these simple tips
textline_cells.orig: Try out a few of these simple tips

textline_cells.text: to help your baby build a strong
textline_cells.orig: to help your baby build a strong

textline_cells.text: foundation of pre-reading skills!
textline_cells.orig: foundation of pre-reading skills!

textline_cells.text: •   A few minutes at a time is just fine! Your little one
textline_cells.orig: •   A few minutes at a time is just fine! Your little one

textline_cells.text: may only be able to sit for a few minutes for a story. Let
textline_cells.orig: may only be able to sit for a few minutes for a story. Let

textline_cells.text: them decide how much time you spend reading. Babies
textline_cells.orig: them decide how much time you spend reading. Babies

textline_cells.text: may just want to play with the book! That's okay. Let your
textline_cells.orig: may just want to play with the book! That's okay. Let your

textline_cells.text: little one experience books the way that interests them.
textline_cells.orig: little one experience books the way that interests them.

textline_cells.text: •  Let your little one practice turning the pages of the
textline_cells.orig: •  Let your little one practice turning the pages of the

textline_cells.text: books you read together. Babies may not be able to turn
textline_cells.orig: books you read together. Babies may not be able to turn

textline_cells.text: the pages of some books yet, but board books are a great
textline_cells.orig: the pages of some books yet, but board books are a great

textline_cells.text: way to start practicing.
textline_cells.orig: way to start practicing.

textline_cells.text: •   Read favorite stories and sing favorite songs over and
textline_cells.orig: •   Read favorite stories and sing favorite songs over and

textline_cells.text: over again. Repeating books and songs will strengthen
textline_cells.orig: over again. Repeating books and songs will strengthen

textline_cells.text: language development and confidence in reading.
textline_cells.orig: language development and confidence in reading.

textline_cells.text: •   Children learn a lot based on your facial expressions
textline_cells.orig: •   Children learn a lot based on your facial expressions

textline_cells.text: and body language. Sometimes sitting face to face while
textline_cells.orig: and body language. Sometimes sitting face to face while

textline_cells.text: reading together helps keep your little one interested.
textline_cells.orig: reading together helps keep your little one interested.

textline_cells.text: •   Point to the words as you read to your baby. This helps
textline_cells.orig: •   Point to the words as you read to your baby. This helps

textline_cells.text: build concept of print and over time, your child will begin
textline_cells.orig: build concept of print and over time, your child will begin

textline_cells.text: to associate print on the page with spoken words.
textline_cells.orig: to associate print on the page with spoken words.

textline_cells.text: •   Sing with your baby! Songs often have notes for every
textline_cells.orig: •   Sing with your baby! Songs often have notes for every

textline_cells.text: syllable, so when you sing songs to your baby, you're
textline_cells.orig: syllable, so when you sing songs to your baby, you're

textline_cells.text: helping them hear that words can be broken into
textline_cells.orig: helping them hear that words can be broken into

textline_cells.text: syllables.
textline_cells.orig: syllables.

textline_cells.text: •   When your baby babbles at you, say something back!
textline_cells.orig: •   When your baby babbles at you, say something back!

textline_cells.text: Then pause and wait for an answer. Don't worry if your
textline_cells.orig: Then pause and wait for an answer. Don't worry if your

textline_cells.text: child can't say anything back, or that you're not
textline_cells.orig: child can't say anything back, or that you're not

textline_cells.text: understanding each other -- your baby is learning how
textline_cells.orig: understanding each other -- your baby is learning how

textline_cells.text: conversations work.
textline_cells.orig: conversations work.

textline_cells.text: •   Singing and clapping to rhymes reinforces the idea
textline_cells.orig: •   Singing and clapping to rhymes reinforces the idea

textline_cells.text: that words are made up of smaller sounds. This will help
textline_cells.orig: that words are made up of smaller sounds. This will help

textline_cells.text: later when they being to sound out words.
textline_cells.orig: later when they being to sound out words.
